task.title: Install Cloud-Native Router on the Managed and Transit Nodes
task.shortdesc: This example procedure shows how to configure the Cloud-Native Routers for management access on the overlay for pre-bound interfaces in a Wind River deployment.

step.cmd: Follow the steps in on the managed node to create virtual functions on the SR-IOV-enabled NIC and pre-bind these interfaces to the vfio DPDK driver. These are ens1f0v0, ens1f0v1, and ens1f0v2 in our example.

info.note: You must create the ens1f0v0 and ens1f0v1 virtual functions from the same physical function or NIC because this solution relies on the virtual Ethernet bridge on the NIC to perform switching between these two virtual functions. In our example, we use the same NIC for all three virtual functions.

step.cmd: Configure and install the Cloud-Native Router on the managed node.
step.info:
Follow the steps in to configure and install the Cloud-Native Router with pre-bound interfaces. Configure the installation Helm chart as you normally would for pre-bound interfaces, but specify the pre-bound interface type for ens1f0v1 as workload and ens1f0v2 as fabric:networkDetails: - ddp: “off” name: ens1f0v1net1 namespace: default type: workload - ddp: “off” name: ens1f0v2net2 namespace: default type: fabricwhere ens1f0v1net1 is the network attachment definition for the ens1f0v1 interface andens1f0v2net2 is the network attachment definition for the ens1f0v2 interface.
The workload interface is the OAM interface.

step.cmd: Configure and install the Cloud-Native Router on the transit node.
step.info: Follow the steps in to configure and install the Cloud-Native Router. Configure the installation Helm chart on the transit node as you normally would (with both interfaces configured as fabric interfaces).fabricInterface: - ens1f0: ddp: "off" - ens1f1: ddp: “off”
